title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 1
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {330} \quad M_{A1B1} = {25.38}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {1159.08} \quad n_{A1B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {442} \quad M_{A1B2} = {34.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {1304.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {591} \quad M_{A2B1} = {45.46}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {659.23} \quad n_{A2B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {474} \quad M_{A2B2} = {36.46}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {1061.23} \quad n_{A2B2} = {13}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {52} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {51}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {52} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {48}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {71739} - \frac{1837^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {71739} - \frac{3374569}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {71739} - {64895.56}\)  \(SS_{total} = {6843.44}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1159.08 + 1304.0 + 659.23 + 1061.23}\)  \(SS_{within} = {4183.54}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {6843.44} - {4183.54}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2659.9}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {772}  \quad n_{A_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1065}  \quad n_{A_2} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{772}^2}{26} + \frac{{1065}^2}{26}} - \frac{{1837}^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{595984}}{26} + \frac{{1134225}}{26}} - \frac{{3374569}}{52}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{22922.46} + {43624.04}} - {64895.56}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {66546.5} - {64895.56}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1650.94}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1650.94}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1650.94}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1650.94}{87.16}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {18.94}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1650.94}{{6843.44} - {0.47} - {1008.49}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1650.94}{5834.48}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.28}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {18.94}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.28}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {921}  \quad n_{B_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {916}  \quad n_{B_2} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{921}^2}{26} + \frac{{916}^2}{26}} - \frac{{1837}^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{848241}}{26} + \frac{{839056}}{26}} - \frac{{3374569}}{52}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{32624.65} + {32271.38}} - {64895.56}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {64896.03} - {64895.56}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {0.47}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.47}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {0.47}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.47}{87.16}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.01}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.47}{{6843.44} - {1650.94} - {1008.49}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.47}{4184.01}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {0.01}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2659.9} - {1650.94} - {0.47}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1008.49}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1008.49}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {1008.49}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{1008.49}{87.16}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {11.57}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1008.49}{{6843.44} - {1650.94} - {0.47}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1008.49}{5192.03}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.19}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {11.57}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.19}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 2
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {259} \quad M_{A1B1} = {23.55}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {102.73} \quad n_{A1B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {221} \quad M_{A1B2} = {20.09}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {62.91} \quad n_{A1B2} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {161} \quad M_{A2B1} = {14.64}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {160.55} \quad n_{A2B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {187} \quad M_{A2B2} = {17.0}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {288.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {11}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {44} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {43}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {44} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {40}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16688} - \frac{828^2}{44}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16688} - \frac{685584}{44}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16688} - {15581.45}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1106.55}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {102.73 + 62.91 + 160.55 + 288.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {614.19}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1106.55} - {614.19}\)  \(SS_{between} = {492.36}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {480}  \quad n_{A_1} = {22}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {348}  \quad n_{A_2} = {22}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.31}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{480}^2}{22} + \frac{{348}^2}{22}} - \frac{{828}^2}{44}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{230400}}{22} + \frac{{121104}}{22}} - \frac{{685584}}{44}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{10472.73} + {5504.73}} - {15581.45}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {15977.46} - {15581.45}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {396.01}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{396.01}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {396.01}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{396.01}{15.35}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {25.8}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{396.01}{{1106.55} - {3.28} - {93.07}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{396.01}{1010.2}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.39}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {40}) = {25.8}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.39}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {420}  \quad n_{B_1} = {22}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {408}  \quad n_{B_2} = {22}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.31}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{420}^2}{22} + \frac{{408}^2}{22}} - \frac{{828}^2}{44}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{176400}}{22} + \frac{{166464}}{22}} - \frac{{685584}}{44}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{8018.18} + {7566.55}} - {15581.45}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {15584.73} - {15581.45}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {3.28}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{3.28}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {3.28}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{3.28}{15.35}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.21}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3.28}{{1106.55} - {396.01} - {93.07}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3.28}{617.47}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {40}) = {0.21}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.31}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {492.36} - {396.01} - {3.28}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {93.07}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{93.07}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {93.07}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{93.07}{15.35}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {6.06}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{93.07}{{1106.55} - {396.01} - {3.28}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{93.07}{707.26}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.13}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {40}) = {6.06}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.13}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 3
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {113} \quad M_{A1B1} = {12.56}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {112.22} \quad n_{A1B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {154} \quad M_{A1B2} = {17.11}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {44.89} \quad n_{A1B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {184} \quad M_{A2B1} = {20.44}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {34.22} \quad n_{A2B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {114} \quad M_{A2B2} = {12.67}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {46.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {128} \quad M_{A3B1} = {14.22}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {37.56} \quad n_{A3B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {153} \quad M_{A3B2} = {17.0}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {20.0} \quad n_{A3B2} = {9}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {54} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {53}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {54} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {48}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13976} - \frac{846^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13976} - \frac{715716}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13976} - {13254.0}\)  \(SS_{total} = {722.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {112.22 + 44.89 + 34.22 + 46.0 + 37.56 + 20.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {294.89}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {722.0} - {294.89}\)  \(SS_{between} = {427.11}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {267}  \quad n_{A_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {298}  \quad n_{A_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {281}  \quad n_{A_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.08}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{267}^2}{18} + \frac{{298}^2}{18} + \frac{{281}^2}{18}} - \frac{{846}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{71289}}{18} + \frac{{88804}}{18} + \frac{{78961}}{18}} - \frac{{715716}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{3960.5} + {4933.56} + {4386.72}} - {13254.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {13280.78} - {13254.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {26.78}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{26.78}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {13.39}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{13.39}{6.14}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {2.18}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{26.78}{{722.0} - {0.29} - {400.04}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{26.78}{321.67}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {2.18}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {425}  \quad n_{B_1} = {27}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {421}  \quad n_{B_2} = {27}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{425}^2}{27} + \frac{{421}^2}{27}} - \frac{{846}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{180625}}{27} + \frac{{177241}}{27}} - \frac{{715716}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{6689.81} + {6564.48}} - {13254.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {13254.29} - {13254.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {0.29}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.29}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {0.29}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.29}{6.14}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.05}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.29}{{722.0} - {26.78} - {400.04}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.29}{295.18}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {0.05}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.08}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {427.11} - {26.78} - {0.29}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {400.04}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{400.04}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {200.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{200.02}{6.14}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {32.58}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{400.04}{{722.0} - {26.78} - {0.29}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{400.04}{694.93}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.58}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {32.58}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.58}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 4
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {159} \quad M_{A1B1} = {31.8}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {52.8} \quad n_{A1B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {126} \quad M_{A1B2} = {25.2}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {26.8} \quad n_{A1B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {126} \quad M_{A1B3} = {25.2}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {6.8} \quad n_{A1B3} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {157} \quad M_{A2B1} = {31.4}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {45.2} \quad n_{A2B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {130} \quad M_{A2B2} = {26.0}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {6.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {117} \quad M_{A2B3} = {23.4}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {33.2} \quad n_{A2B3} = {5}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {156} \quad M_{A3B1} = {31.2}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {68.8} \quad n_{A3B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {171} \quad M_{A3B2} = {34.2}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {58.8} \quad n_{A3B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {161} \quad M_{A3B3} = {32.2}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {24.8} \quad n_{A3B3} = {5}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {45} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {44}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {45} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {36}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38677} - \frac{1303^2}{45}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38677} - \frac{1697809}{45}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38677} - {37729.09}\)  \(SS_{total} = {947.91}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {52.8 + 26.8 + 6.8 + 45.2 + 6.0 + 33.2 + 68.8 + 58.8 + 24.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = {323.2}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {947.91} - {323.2}\)  \(SS_{between} = {624.71}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {411}  \quad n_{A_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {404}  \quad n_{A_2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {488}  \quad n_{A_3} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{411}^2}{15} + \frac{{404}^2}{15} + \frac{{488}^2}{15}} - \frac{{1303}^2}{45}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{168921}}{15} + \frac{{163216}}{15} + \frac{{238144}}{15}} - \frac{{1697809}}{45}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{11261.4} + {10881.07} + {15876.27}} - {37729.09}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {38018.74} - {37729.09}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {289.65}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{289.65}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {144.82}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{144.82}{8.98}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {16.13}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{289.65}{{947.91} - {159.52} - {175.54}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{289.65}{612.85}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.47}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {16.13}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.47}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {472}  \quad n_{B_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {427}  \quad n_{B_2} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {404}  \quad n_{B_3} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{472}^2}{15} + \frac{{427}^2}{15} + \frac{{404}^2}{15}} - \frac{{1303}^2}{45}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{222784}}{15} + \frac{{182329}}{15} + \frac{{163216}}{15}} - \frac{{1697809}}{45}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{14852.27} + {12155.27} + {10881.07}} - {37729.09}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {37888.61} - {37729.09}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {159.52}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{159.52}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {79.76}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{79.76}{8.98}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {8.88}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{159.52}{{947.91} - {289.65} - {175.54}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{159.52}{482.72}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.33}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {8.88}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.33}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.63}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {624.71} - {289.65} - {159.52}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {175.54}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{175.54}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {43.88}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{43.88}{8.98}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.89}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{175.54}{{947.91} - {289.65} - {159.52}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{175.54}{498.74}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.35}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {36}) = {4.89}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.35}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13260} - \frac{1040^2}{84}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13260} - \frac{1081600}{84}\)  \(SS_{total} = {13260} - {12876.19}\)  \(SS_{total} = {383.81}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {50.86 + 36.86 + 48.36 + 69.21 + 30.86 + 39.43}\)  \(SS_{within} = {275.58}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {383.81} - {275.58}\)  \(SS_{between} = {108.23}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {336}  \quad n_{A_1} = {28}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {342}  \quad n_{A_2} = {28}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {362}  \quad n_{A_3} = {28}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{336}^2}{28} + \frac{{342}^2}{28} + \frac{{362}^2}{28}} - \frac{{1040}^2}{84}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{112896}}{28} + \frac{{116964}}{28} + \frac{{131044}}{28}} - \frac{{1081600}}{84}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{4032.0} + {4177.29} + {4680.14}} - {12876.19}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {12889.43} - {12876.19}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {13.24}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{13.24}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {6.62}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{6.62}{3.53}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.88}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{13.24}{{383.81} - {17.19} - {77.8}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{13.24}{288.82}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.05}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {78}) = {1.88}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.05}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {501}  \quad n_{B_1} = {42}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {539}  \quad n_{B_2} = {42}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.96}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{501}^2}{42} + \frac{{539}^2}{42}} - \frac{{1040}^2}{84}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{251001}}{42} + \frac{{290521}}{42}} - \frac{{1081600}}{84}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{5976.21} + {6917.17}} - {12876.19}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {12893.38} - {12876.19}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {17.19}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{17.19}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {17.19}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{17.19}{{383.81} - {13.24} - {77.8}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{17.19}{292.77}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {78}) = {4.87}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {108.23} - {13.24} - {17.19}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {77.8}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{77.8}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {38.9}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{38.9}{3.53}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {11.02}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{77.8}{{383.81} - {13.24} - {17.19}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{77.8}{353.38}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.22}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {78}) = {11.02}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.22}\)

Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {241} \quad M_{A1B1} = {48.2}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {152.8} \quad n_{A1B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {215} \quad M_{A1B2} = {43.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {494.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {255} \quad M_{A1B3} = {51.0}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {186.0} \quad n_{A1B3} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {197} \quad M_{A2B1} = {39.4}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {219.2} \quad n_{A2B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {226} \quad M_{A2B2} = {45.2}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {94.8} \quad n_{A2B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {265} \quad M_{A2B3} = {53.0}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {182.0} \quad n_{A2B3} = {5}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {30} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {29}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {30} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {24}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {67217} - \frac{1399^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {67217} - \frac{1957201}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {67217} - {65240.03}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1976.97}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {152.8 + 494.0 + 186.0 + 219.2 + 94.8 + 182.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1328.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1976.97} - {1328.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = {648.17}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {711}  \quad n_{A_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {688}  \quad n_{A_2} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{711}^2}{15} + \frac{{688}^2}{15}} - \frac{{1399}^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{505521}}{15} + \frac{{473344}}{15}} - \frac{{1957201}}{30}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{33701.4} + {31556.27}} - {65240.03}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {65257.67} - {65240.03}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {17.64}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{17.64}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {17.64}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{17.64}{55.37}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.32}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{17.64}{{1976.97} - {432.47} - {198.06}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{17.64}{1346.44}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {0.32}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {438}  \quad n_{B_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {441}  \quad n_{B_2} = {10}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {520}  \quad n_{B_3} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.4}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{438}^2}{10} + \frac{{441}^2}{10} + \frac{{520}^2}{10}} - \frac{{1399}^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{191844}}{10} + \frac{{194481}}{10} + \frac{{270400}}{10}} - \frac{{1957201}}{30}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{19184.4} + {19448.1} + {27040.0}} - {65240.03}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {65672.5} - {65240.03}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {432.47}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{432.47}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {216.24}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{216.24}{55.37}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {3.91}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{432.47}{{1976.97} - {17.64} - {198.06}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{432.47}{1761.27}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.25}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {3.91}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.25}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.4}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {648.17} - {17.64} - {432.47}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {198.06}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{198.06}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {99.03}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{99.03}{55.37}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {1.79}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{198.06}{{1976.97} - {17.64} - {432.47}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{198.06}{1526.86}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.13}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {1.79}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.13}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {23748} - \frac{1204^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{total} = {23748} - \frac{1449616}{63}\)  \(SS_{total} = {23748} - {23009.78}\)  \(SS_{total} = {738.22}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {37.43 + 32.86 + 26.0 + 19.43 + 11.71 + 7.43 + 19.71 + 21.43 + 11.71}\)  \(SS_{within} = {187.71}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {738.22} - {187.71}\)  \(SS_{between} = {550.51}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{125.4}{3.48}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {36.03}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{250.79}{{738.22} - {48.22} - {251.5}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{250.79}{438.5}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.57}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {36.03}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.57}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {409}  \quad n_{B_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {419}  \quad n_{B_2} = {21}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {376}  \quad n_{B_3} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.17}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{409}^2}{21} + \frac{{419}^2}{21} + \frac{{376}^2}{21}} - \frac{{1204}^2}{63}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{167281}}{21} + \frac{{175561}}{21} + \frac{{141376}}{21}} - \frac{{1449616}}{63}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{7965.76} + {8360.05} + {6732.19}} - {23009.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {23058.0} - {23009.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {48.22}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{48.22}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {24.11}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{24.11}{3.48}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {6.93}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{48.22}{{738.22} - {250.79} - {251.5}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{48.22}{235.93}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.2}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {6.93}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.2}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.54}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {550.51} - {250.79} - {48.22}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {251.5}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{251.5}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {62.88}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{62.88}{3.48}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {18.07}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{251.5}{{738.22} - {250.79} - {48.22}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{251.5}{439.21}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.57}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {54}) = {18.07}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.57}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {118369} - \frac{2487^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {118369} - \frac{6185169}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {118369} - {114540.17}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3828.83}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {305.56 + 338.89 + 272.22 + 314.0 + 347.56 + 453.56}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2031.79}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3828.83} - {2031.79}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1797.04}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{323.17}{42.33}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {7.63}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{646.34}{{3828.83} - {220.02} - {930.68}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{646.34}{2678.13}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.24}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {7.63}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.24}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1189}  \quad n_{B_1} = {27}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1298}  \quad n_{B_2} = {27}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1189}^2}{27} + \frac{{1298}^2}{27}} - \frac{{2487}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1413721}}{27} + \frac{{1684804}}{27}} - \frac{{6185169}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{52360.04} + {62400.15}} - {114540.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {114760.19} - {114540.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {220.02}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{220.02}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {220.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{220.02}{42.33}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {5.2}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{220.02}{{3828.83} - {646.34} - {930.68}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{220.02}{2251.81}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {5.2}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.08}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1797.04} - {646.34} - {220.02}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {930.68}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{930.68}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {465.34}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{465.34}{42.33}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {10.99}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{930.68}{{3828.83} - {646.34} - {220.02}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{930.68}{2962.47}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {10.99}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\)

Problem 9
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {176} \quad M_{A1B1} = {35.2}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {52.8} \quad n_{A1B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {125} \quad M_{A1B2} = {25.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {122.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {152} \quad M_{A2B1} = {30.4}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {77.2} \quad n_{A2B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {153} \quad M_{A2B2} = {30.6}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {45.2} \quad n_{A2B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {208} \quad M_{A3B1} = {41.6}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {39.2} \quad n_{A3B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {179} \quad M_{A3B2} = {35.8}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {32.8} \quad n_{A3B2} = {5}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {30} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {29}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {30} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {24}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {34053} - \frac{993^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {34053} - \frac{986049}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {34053} - {32868.3}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1184.7}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {52.8 + 122.0 + 77.2 + 45.2 + 39.2 + 32.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = {369.2}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1184.7} - {369.2}\)  \(SS_{between} = {815.5}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {301}  \quad n_{A_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {305}  \quad n_{A_2} = {10}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {387}  \quad n_{A_3} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.4}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{301}^2}{10} + \frac{{305}^2}{10} + \frac{{387}^2}{10}} - \frac{{993}^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{90601}}{10} + \frac{{93025}}{10} + \frac{{149769}}{10}} - \frac{{986049}}{30}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{9060.1} + {9302.5} + {14976.9}} - {32868.3}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {33339.5} - {32868.3}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {471.2}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{471.2}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {235.6}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{235.6}{15.38}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {15.32}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{471.2}{{1184.7} - {208.04} - {136.26}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{471.2}{840.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.56}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {15.32}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.56}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {536}  \quad n_{B_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {457}  \quad n_{B_2} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{536}^2}{15} + \frac{{457}^2}{15}} - \frac{{993}^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{287296}}{15} + \frac{{208849}}{15}} - \frac{{986049}}{30}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{19153.07} + {13923.27}} - {32868.3}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {33076.34} - {32868.3}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {208.04}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{208.04}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {208.04}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{208.04}{15.38}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {13.53}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{208.04}{{1184.7} - {471.2} - {136.26}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{208.04}{577.24}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.36}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {13.53}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.36}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.4}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {815.5} - {471.2} - {208.04}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {136.26}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{136.26}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {68.13}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{68.13}{15.38}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.43}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{136.26}{{1184.7} - {471.2} - {208.04}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{136.26}{505.46}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.27}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {4.43}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.27}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 10
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {284} \quad M_{A1B1} = {35.5}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {258.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {211} \quad M_{A1B2} = {26.38}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {99.88} \quad n_{A1B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {232} \quad M_{A2B1} = {29.0}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {176.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {275} \quad M_{A2B2} = {34.38}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {457.88} \quad n_{A2B2} = {8}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {32} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {31}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {32} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {28}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {32820} - \frac{1002^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {32820} - \frac{1004004}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {32820} - {31375.12}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1444.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {258.0 + 99.88 + 176.0 + 457.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = {991.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1444.88} - {991.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = {453.12}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {495}  \quad n_{A_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {507}  \quad n_{A_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{495}^2}{16} + \frac{{507}^2}{16}} - \frac{{1002}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{245025}}{16} + \frac{{257049}}{16}} - \frac{{1004004}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{15314.06} + {16065.56}} - {31375.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {31379.62} - {31375.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {4.5}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{4.5}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {4.5}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{4.5}{35.42}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.13}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{4.5}{{1444.88} - {28.13} - {420.49}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{4.5}{996.26}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {0.13}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {516}  \quad n_{B_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {486}  \quad n_{B_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{516}^2}{16} + \frac{{486}^2}{16}} - \frac{{1002}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{266256}}{16} + \frac{{236196}}{16}} - \frac{{1004004}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{16641.0} + {14762.25}} - {31375.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {31403.25} - {31375.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {28.13}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{28.13}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {28.13}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{28.13}{35.42}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.79}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{28.13}{{1444.88} - {4.5} - {420.49}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{28.13}{1019.89}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {0.79}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {453.12} - {4.5} - {28.13}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {420.49}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{420.49}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {420.49}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{420.49}{35.42}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {11.87}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{420.49}{{1444.88} - {4.5} - {28.13}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{420.49}{1412.25}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.3}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {11.87}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.3}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 11
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {152} \quad M_{A1B1} = {19.0}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {326.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {213} \quad M_{A1B2} = {26.62}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {137.88} \quad n_{A1B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {163} \quad M_{A2B1} = {20.38}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {79.88} \quad n_{A2B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {155} \quad M_{A2B2} = {19.38}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {109.88} \quad n_{A2B2} = {8}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {32} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {31}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {32} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {28}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15537} - \frac{683^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15537} - \frac{466489}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15537} - {14577.78}\)  \(SS_{total} = {959.22}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {326.0 + 137.88 + 79.88 + 109.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = {653.64}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {959.22} - {653.64}\)  \(SS_{between} = {305.58}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {365}  \quad n_{A_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {318}  \quad n_{A_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{365}^2}{16} + \frac{{318}^2}{16}} - \frac{{683}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{133225}}{16} + \frac{{101124}}{16}} - \frac{{466489}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{8326.56} + {6320.25}} - {14577.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {14646.81} - {14577.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {69.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{69.03}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {69.03}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{69.03}{23.34}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {2.96}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{69.03}{{959.22} - {87.78} - {148.77}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{69.03}{722.67}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {2.96}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {315}  \quad n_{B_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {368}  \quad n_{B_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{315}^2}{16} + \frac{{368}^2}{16}} - \frac{{683}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{99225}}{16} + \frac{{135424}}{16}} - \frac{{466489}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{6201.56} + {8464.0}} - {14577.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {14665.56} - {14577.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {87.78}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{87.78}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {87.78}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{87.78}{23.34}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {3.76}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{87.78}{{959.22} - {69.03} - {148.77}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{87.78}{741.42}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.12}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {3.76}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.12}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {305.58} - {69.03} - {87.78}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {148.77}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{148.77}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {148.77}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{148.77}{23.34}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {6.37}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{148.77}{{959.22} - {69.03} - {87.78}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{148.77}{802.41}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.19}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {6.37}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.19}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 12
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {396} \quad M_{A1B1} = {56.57}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {213.71} \quad n_{A1B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {381} \quad M_{A1B2} = {54.43}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {897.71} \quad n_{A1B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {375} \quad M_{A2B1} = {53.57}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {421.71} \quad n_{A2B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {372} \quad M_{A2B2} = {53.14}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {776.86} \quad n_{A2B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {458} \quad M_{A3B1} = {65.43}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {1173.71} \quad n_{A3B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {452} \quad M_{A3B2} = {64.57}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {197.71} \quad n_{A3B2} = {7}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {42} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {41}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {42} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {36}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {145832} - \frac{2434^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{total} = {145832} - \frac{5924356}{42}\)  \(SS_{total} = {145832} - {141056.1}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4775.9}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {213.71 + 897.71 + 421.71 + 776.86 + 1173.71 + 197.71}\)  \(SS_{within} = {3681.41}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {4775.9} - {3681.41}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1094.49}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {777}  \quad n_{A_1} = {14}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {747}  \quad n_{A_2} = {14}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {910}  \quad n_{A_3} = {14}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{777}^2}{14} + \frac{{747}^2}{14} + \frac{{910}^2}{14}} - \frac{{2434}^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{603729}}{14} + \frac{{558009}}{14} + \frac{{828100}}{14}} - \frac{{5924356}}{42}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{43123.5} + {39857.79} + {59150.0}} - {141056.1}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {142131.29} - {141056.1}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1075.19}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1075.19}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {537.6}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{537.6}{102.26}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {5.26}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1075.19}{{4775.9} - {13.71} - {5.59}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1075.19}{4756.6}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.23}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {5.26}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.23}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1229}  \quad n_{B_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1205}  \quad n_{B_2} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1229}^2}{21} + \frac{{1205}^2}{21}} - \frac{{2434}^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1510441}}{21} + \frac{{1452025}}{21}} - \frac{{5924356}}{42}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{71925.76} + {69144.05}} - {141056.1}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {141069.81} - {141056.1}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {13.71}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{13.71}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {13.71}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{13.71}{102.26}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.13}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{13.71}{{4775.9} - {1075.19} - {5.59}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{13.71}{3695.12}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {0.13}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1094.49} - {1075.19} - {13.71}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {5.59}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{5.59}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {2.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{2.8}{102.26}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.03}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5.59}{{4775.9} - {1075.19} - {13.71}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5.59}{3687.0}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {0.03}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)

Problem 13
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {72} \quad M_{A1B1} = {9.0}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {40.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {106} \quad M_{A1B2} = {13.25}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {23.5} \quad n_{A1B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {97} \quad M_{A2B1} = {12.12}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {12.88} \quad n_{A2B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {127} \quad M_{A2B2} = {15.88}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {28.88} \quad n_{A2B2} = {8}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {32} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {31}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {32} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {28}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {5350} - \frac{402^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {5350} - \frac{161604}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {5350} - {5050.12}\)  \(SS_{total} = {299.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {40.0 + 23.5 + 12.88 + 28.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = {105.26}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {299.88} - {105.26}\)  \(SS_{between} = {194.62}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {178}  \quad n_{A_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {224}  \quad n_{A_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{178}^2}{16} + \frac{{224}^2}{16}} - \frac{{402}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{31684}}{16} + \frac{{50176}}{16}} - \frac{{161604}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{1980.25} + {3136.0}} - {5050.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {5116.25} - {5050.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {66.13}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{66.13}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {66.13}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{66.13}{3.76}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {17.59}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{66.13}{{299.88} - {128.0} - {0.49}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{66.13}{171.39}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.39}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {17.59}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.39}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {169}  \quad n_{B_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {233}  \quad n_{B_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{169}^2}{16} + \frac{{233}^2}{16}} - \frac{{402}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{28561}}{16} + \frac{{54289}}{16}} - \frac{{161604}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{1785.06} + {3393.06}} - {5050.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {5178.12} - {5050.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {128.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{128.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {128.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{128.0}{3.76}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {34.04}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{128.0}{{299.88} - {66.13} - {0.49}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{128.0}{233.26}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.55}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {34.04}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.55}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {194.62} - {66.13} - {128.0}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {0.49}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{0.49}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {0.49}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{0.49}{3.76}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.13}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.49}{{299.88} - {66.13} - {128.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.49}{105.75}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {0.13}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {21614} - \frac{792^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {21614} - \frac{627264}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {21614} - {19602.0}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2012.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {254.0 + 215.88 + 168.0 + 653.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1291.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2012.0} - {1291.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = {720.24}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {351}  \quad n_{A_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {441}  \quad n_{A_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{351}^2}{16} + \frac{{441}^2}{16}} - \frac{{792}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{123201}}{16} + \frac{{194481}}{16}} - \frac{{627264}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{7700.06} + {12155.06}} - {19602.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {19855.12} - {19602.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {253.12}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{253.12}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {253.12}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{253.12}{46.13}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {5.49}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{253.12}{{2012.0} - {2.0} - {465.12}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{253.12}{1544.88}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {5.49}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {392}  \quad n_{B_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {400}  \quad n_{B_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{392}^2}{16} + \frac{{400}^2}{16}} - \frac{{792}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{153664}}{16} + \frac{{160000}}{16}} - \frac{{627264}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{9604.0} + {10000.0}} - {19602.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {19604.0} - {19602.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {2.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{2.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {2.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{2.0}{46.13}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.04}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.0}{{2012.0} - {253.12} - {465.12}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.0}{1293.76}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {0.04}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {720.24} - {253.12} - {2.0}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {465.12}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{465.12}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {465.12}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{465.12}{46.13}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {10.08}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{465.12}{{2012.0} - {253.12} - {2.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{465.12}{1756.88}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.26}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {10.08}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.26}\)
