Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {32304} - \frac{1988^2}{126}\)  \(SS_{total} = {32304} - \frac{3952144}{126}\)  \(SS_{total} = {32304} - {31366.22}\)  \(SS_{total} = {937.78}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {47.43 + 56.36 + 62.86 + 35.43 + 79.5 + 51.21 + 36.0 + 53.71 + 64.93}\)  \(SS_{within} = {487.43}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {937.78} - {487.43}\)  \(SS_{between} = {450.35}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {609}  \quad n_{A_1} = {42}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {734}  \quad n_{A_2} = {42}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {645}  \quad n_{A_3} = {42}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.79}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{609}^2}{42} + \frac{{734}^2}{42} + \frac{{645}^2}{42}} - \frac{{1988}^2}{126}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{370881}}{42} + \frac{{538756}}{42} + \frac{{416025}}{42}} - \frac{{3952144}}{126}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{8830.5} + {12827.52} + {9905.36}} - {31366.22}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {31563.38} - {31366.22}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {197.16}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{197.16}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {98.58}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{98.58}{4.17}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {23.64}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{197.16}{{937.78} - {14.17} - {239.02}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{197.16}{684.59}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {117}) = {23.64}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {644}  \quad n_{B_1} = {42}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {678}  \quad n_{B_2} = {42}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {666}  \quad n_{B_3} = {42}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.79}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{644}^2}{42} + \frac{{678}^2}{42} + \frac{{666}^2}{42}} - \frac{{1988}^2}{126}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{414736}}{42} + \frac{{459684}}{42} + \frac{{443556}}{42}} - \frac{{3952144}}{126}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{9874.67} + {10944.86} + {10560.86}} - {31366.22}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {31380.39} - {31366.22}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {14.17}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{14.17}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {7.08}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{7.08}{4.17}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {1.7}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{14.17}{{937.78} - {197.16} - {239.02}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{14.17}{501.6}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {117}) = {1.7}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{410.04}{20.28}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {20.22}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{820.07}{{2437.99} - {136.12} - {143.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{820.07}{2158.5}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.38}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {20.22}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.38}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{169.73}{43.63}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {3.89}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{339.46}{{4955.6} - {421.06} - {660.68}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{339.46}{3873.86}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {81}) = {3.89}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.56}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1421.2} - {421.06} - {339.46}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {660.68}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{660.68}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {165.17}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{165.17}{43.63}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {3.79}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{660.68}{{4955.6} - {421.06} - {339.46}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{660.68}{4195.08}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {81}) = {3.79}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.16}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {52219} - \frac{1089^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{total} = {52219} - \frac{1185921}{24}\)  \(SS_{total} = {52219} - {49413.38}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2805.62}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {247.33 + 63.33 + 193.33 + 898.83}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1402.82}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2805.62} - {1402.82}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1402.8}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{7.03}{70.14}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.1}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{7.03}{{2805.62} - {0.37} - {1395.4}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{7.03}{1409.85}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {0.1}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {546}  \quad n_{B_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {543}  \quad n_{B_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.1}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{546}^2}{12} + \frac{{543}^2}{12}} - \frac{{1089}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{298116}}{12} + \frac{{294849}}{12}} - \frac{{1185921}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{24843.0} + {24570.75}} - {49413.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {49413.75} - {49413.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {0.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.37}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {0.37}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.37}{70.14}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.01}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.37}{{2805.62} - {7.03} - {1395.4}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.37}{1403.19}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {0.01}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.1}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1402.8} - {7.03} - {0.37}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1395.4}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1395.4}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {1395.4}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{1395.4}{70.14}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {19.89}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1395.4}{{2805.62} - {7.03} - {0.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1395.4}{2798.22}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.5}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {19.89}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.5}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {99141} - \frac{2839^2}{84}\)  \(SS_{total} = {99141} - \frac{8059921}{84}\)  \(SS_{total} = {99141} - {95951.44}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3189.56}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {134.93 + 106.0 + 138.36 + 171.5 + 324.86 + 149.43}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1025.08}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3189.56} - {1025.08}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2164.48}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1542}  \quad n_{A_1} = {42}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1297}  \quad n_{A_2} = {42}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.96}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1542}^2}{42} + \frac{{1297}^2}{42}} - \frac{{2839}^2}{84}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2377764}}{42} + \frac{{1682209}}{42}} - \frac{{8059921}}{84}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{56613.43} + {40052.6}} - {95951.44}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {96666.03} - {95951.44}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {714.59}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{714.59}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {714.59}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{714.59}{13.14}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {54.38}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{714.59}{{3189.56} - {62.31} - {1387.58}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{714.59}{1739.67}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.41}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {78}) = {54.38}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.41}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {916}  \quad n_{B_1} = {28}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {948}  \quad n_{B_2} = {28}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {975}  \quad n_{B_3} = {28}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{916}^2}{28} + \frac{{948}^2}{28} + \frac{{975}^2}{28}} - \frac{{2839}^2}{84}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{839056}}{28} + \frac{{898704}}{28} + \frac{{950625}}{28}} - \frac{{8059921}}{84}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{29966.29} + {32096.57} + {33950.89}} - {95951.44}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {96013.75} - {95951.44}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {62.31}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{62.31}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {31.16}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{31.16}{13.14}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {2.37}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{62.31}{{3189.56} - {714.59} - {1387.58}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{62.31}{1087.39}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {78}) = {2.37}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2164.48} - {714.59} - {62.31}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1387.58}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1387.58}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {693.79}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{693.79}{13.14}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {52.8}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1387.58}{{3189.56} - {714.59} - {62.31}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1387.58}{2412.66}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.58}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {78}) = {52.8}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.58}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {148260} - \frac{2930^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{total} = {148260} - \frac{8584900}{60}\)  \(SS_{total} = {148260} - {143081.67}\)  \(SS_{total} = {5178.33}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {555.73 + 615.73 + 613.73 + 597.73}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2382.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {5178.33} - {2382.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2795.41}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1524}  \quad n_{A_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1406}  \quad n_{A_2} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1524}^2}{30} + \frac{{1406}^2}{30}} - \frac{{2930}^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2322576}}{30} + \frac{{1976836}}{30}} - \frac{{8584900}}{60}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{77419.2} + {65894.53}} - {143081.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {143313.73} - {143081.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {232.06}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{232.06}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {232.06}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{232.06}{42.55}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {5.45}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{232.06}{{5178.33} - {281.66} - {2281.69}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{232.06}{2614.98}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {5.45}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1530}  \quad n_{B_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1400}  \quad n_{B_2} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1530}^2}{30} + \frac{{1400}^2}{30}} - \frac{{2930}^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2340900}}{30} + \frac{{1960000}}{30}} - \frac{{8584900}}{60}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{78030.0} + {65333.33}} - {143081.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {143363.33} - {143081.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {281.66}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{281.66}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {281.66}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{281.66}{42.55}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {6.62}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{281.66}{{5178.33} - {232.06} - {2281.69}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{281.66}{2664.58}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.11}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {6.62}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.11}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2795.41} - {232.06} - {281.66}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {2281.69}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{2281.69}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {2281.69}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{2281.69}{42.55}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {53.62}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2281.69}{{5178.33} - {232.06} - {281.66}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2281.69}{4664.61}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.49}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {53.62}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.49}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 21
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {74} \quad M_{A1B1} = {12.33}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {17.33} \quad n_{A1B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {85} \quad M_{A1B2} = {14.17}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {48.83} \quad n_{A1B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {99} \quad M_{A2B1} = {16.5}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {89.5} \quad n_{A2B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {80} \quad M_{A2B2} = {13.33}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {23.33} \quad n_{A2B2} = {6}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {24} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {23}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {24} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {20}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4996} - \frac{338^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4996} - \frac{114244}{24}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4996} - {4760.17}\)  \(SS_{total} = {235.83}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {17.33 + 48.83 + 89.5 + 23.33}\)  \(SS_{within} = {178.99}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {235.83} - {178.99}\)  \(SS_{between} = {56.84}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {159}  \quad n_{A_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {179}  \quad n_{A_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{159}^2}{12} + \frac{{179}^2}{12}} - \frac{{338}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{25281}}{12} + \frac{{32041}}{12}} - \frac{{114244}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{2106.75} + {2670.08}} - {4760.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {4776.83} - {4760.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {16.66}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{16.66}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {16.66}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{16.66}{8.95}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.86}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{16.66}{{235.83} - {2.66} - {37.52}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{16.66}{195.65}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {1.86}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {173}  \quad n_{B_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {165}  \quad n_{B_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{173}^2}{12} + \frac{{165}^2}{12}} - \frac{{338}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{29929}}{12} + \frac{{27225}}{12}} - \frac{{114244}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{2494.08} + {2268.75}} - {4760.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {4762.83} - {4760.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {2.66}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{2.66}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {2.66}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{2.66}{8.95}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.3}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.66}{{235.83} - {16.66} - {37.52}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.66}{181.65}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {0.3}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {56.84} - {16.66} - {2.66}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {37.52}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{37.52}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {37.52}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{37.52}{8.95}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.19}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{37.52}{{235.83} - {16.66} - {2.66}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{37.52}{216.51}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {4.19}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.17}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 22
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {213} \quad M_{A1B1} = {19.36}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {226.55} \quad n_{A1B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {236} \quad M_{A1B2} = {21.45}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {614.73} \quad n_{A1B2} = {11}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {179} \quad M_{A1B3} = {16.27}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {264.18} \quad n_{A1B3} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {308} \quad M_{A2B1} = {28.0}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {328.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {281} \quad M_{A2B2} = {25.55}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {460.73} \quad n_{A2B2} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {326} \quad M_{A2B3} = {29.64}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {350.55} \quad n_{A2B3} = {11}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {66} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {65}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {66} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {60}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {39809} - \frac{1543^2}{66}\)  \(SS_{total} = {39809} - \frac{2380849}{66}\)  \(SS_{total} = {39809} - {36073.47}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3735.53}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {226.55 + 614.73 + 264.18 + 328.0 + 460.73 + 350.55}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2244.74}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3735.53} - {2244.74}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1490.79}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {628}  \quad n_{A_1} = {33}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {915}  \quad n_{A_2} = {33}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.08}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{628}^2}{33} + \frac{{915}^2}{33}} - \frac{{1543}^2}{66}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{394384}}{33} + \frac{{837225}}{33}} - \frac{{2380849}}{66}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{11951.03} + {25370.45}} - {36073.47}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {37321.48} - {36073.47}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1248.01}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1248.01}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1248.01}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1248.01}{37.41}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {33.36}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1248.01}{{3735.53} - {6.31} - {236.47}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1248.01}{3492.75}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.36}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {60}) = {33.36}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.36}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {521}  \quad n_{B_1} = {22}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {517}  \quad n_{B_2} = {22}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {505}  \quad n_{B_3} = {22}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.98}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{521}^2}{22} + \frac{{517}^2}{22} + \frac{{505}^2}{22}} - \frac{{1543}^2}{66}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{271441}}{22} + \frac{{267289}}{22} + \frac{{255025}}{22}} - \frac{{2380849}}{66}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{12338.23} + {12149.5} + {11592.05}} - {36073.47}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {36079.78} - {36073.47}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {6.31}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{6.31}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {3.16}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{3.16}{37.41}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.08}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{6.31}{{3735.53} - {1248.01} - {236.47}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{6.31}{2251.05}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {60}) = {0.08}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.98}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1490.79} - {1248.01} - {6.31}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {236.47}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{236.47}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {118.24}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{118.24}{37.41}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {3.16}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{236.47}{{3735.53} - {1248.01} - {6.31}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{236.47}{2481.21}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {60}) = {3.16}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA

Problem 23

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1129.33}{{4237.95} - {522.66} - {1292.7}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1129.33}{2422.59}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.47}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {78}) = {68.11}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.47}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1242}  \quad n_{B_1} = {28}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1354}  \quad n_{B_2} = {28}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {1186}  \quad n_{B_3} = {28}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1242}^2}{28} + \frac{{1354}^2}{28} + \frac{{1186}^2}{28}} - \frac{{3782}^2}{84}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1542564}}{28} + \frac{{1833316}}{28} + \frac{{1406596}}{28}} - \frac{{14303524}}{84}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{55091.57} + {65475.57} + {50235.57}} - {170280.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {170802.71} - {170280.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {522.66}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{522.66}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {261.33}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{261.33}{16.58}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {15.76}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{522.66}{{4237.95} - {1129.33} - {1292.7}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{522.66}{1815.92}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {78}) = {15.76}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.29}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {96881} - \frac{1827^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {96881} - \frac{3337929}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {96881} - {92720.25}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4160.75}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {229.33 + 109.33 + 51.33 + 750.83 + 57.33 + 763.33}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1961.48}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {4160.75} - {1961.48}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2199.27}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{155.16}{{4160.75} - {491.37} - {1552.74}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{155.16}{2116.64}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.07}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {30}) = {1.19}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.07}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {980}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {847}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.56}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{980}^2}{18} + \frac{{847}^2}{18}} - \frac{{1827}^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{960400}}{18} + \frac{{717409}}{18}} - \frac{{3337929}}{36}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{53355.56} + {39856.06}} - {92720.25}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {93211.62} - {92720.25}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {491.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{491.37}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {491.37}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{491.37}{65.38}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {7.52}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{491.37}{{4160.75} - {155.16} - {1552.74}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{491.37}{2452.85}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.2}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {30}) = {7.52}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.2}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.39}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2199.27} - {155.16} - {491.37}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1552.74}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1552.74}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {776.37}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{776.37}{65.38}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {11.87}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1552.74}{{4160.75} - {155.16} - {491.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1552.74}{3514.22}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.44}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {30}) = {11.87}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.44}\)

Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {302} \quad M_{A1B1} = {50.33}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {121.33} \quad n_{A1B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {353} \quad M_{A1B2} = {58.83}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {134.83} \quad n_{A1B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {304} \quad M_{A2B1} = {50.67}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {75.33} \quad n_{A2B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {325} \quad M_{A2B2} = {54.17}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {158.83} \quad n_{A2B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {299} \quad M_{A3B1} = {49.83}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {450.83} \quad n_{A3B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {246} \quad M_{A3B2} = {41.0}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {388.0} \quad n_{A3B2} = {6}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {36} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {35}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {36} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {30}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {95291} - \frac{1829^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {95291} - \frac{3345241}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {95291} - {92923.36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2367.64}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {121.33 + 134.83 + 75.33 + 158.83 + 450.83 + 388.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1329.15}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2367.64} - {1329.15}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1038.49}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{275.44}{44.3}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {6.22}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{550.88}{{2367.64} - {10.03} - {477.58}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{550.88}{1880.03}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {30}) = {6.22}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {905}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {924}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.17}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{905}^2}{18} + \frac{{924}^2}{18}} - \frac{{1829}^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{819025}}{18} + \frac{{853776}}{18}} - \frac{{3345241}}{36}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{45501.39} + {47432.0}} - {92923.36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {92933.39} - {92923.36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {10.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{10.03}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {10.03}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{10.03}{44.3}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.23}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{10.03}{{2367.64} - {550.88} - {477.58}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{10.03}{1339.18}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {30}) = {0.23}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.32}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1038.49} - {550.88} - {10.03}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {477.58}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{477.58}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {238.79}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{238.79}{44.3}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {5.39}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{477.58}{{2367.64} - {550.88} - {10.03}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{477.58}{1806.73}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.26}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {30}) = {5.39}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.26}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{22.24}{{1407.08} - {398.77} - {480.07}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{22.24}{528.24}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {2.11}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.04}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {901.08} - {398.77} - {22.24}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {480.07}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{480.07}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {480.07}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{480.07}{10.54}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {45.55}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{480.07}{{1407.08} - {398.77} - {22.24}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{480.07}{986.07}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.49}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {45.55}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.49}\)

text:

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {79680} - \frac{1760^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{total} = {79680} - \frac{3097600}{40}\)  \(SS_{total} = {79680} - {77440.0}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2240.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {280.5 + 338.4 + 272.0 + 400.9}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1291.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2240.0} - {1291.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = {948.2}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {939}  \quad n_{A_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {821}  \quad n_{A_2} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.4}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{939}^2}{20} + \frac{{821}^2}{20}} - \frac{{1760}^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{881721}}{20} + \frac{{674041}}{20}} - \frac{{3097600}}{40}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{44086.05} + {33702.05}} - {77440.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {77788.1} - {77440.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {348.1}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{348.1}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {348.1}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{348.1}{35.88}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {9.7}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{348.1}{{2240.0} - {22.5} - {577.6}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{348.1}{1639.9}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.21}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {9.7}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.21}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {895}  \quad n_{B_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {865}  \quad n_{B_2} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.4}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{895}^2}{20} + \frac{{865}^2}{20}} - \frac{{1760}^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{801025}}{20} + \frac{{748225}}{20}} - \frac{{3097600}}{40}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{40051.25} + {37411.25}} - {77440.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {77462.5} - {77440.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {22.5}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{22.5}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {22.5}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{22.5}{35.88}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.63}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{22.5}{{2240.0} - {348.1} - {577.6}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{22.5}{1314.3}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {0.63}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.4}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {948.2} - {348.1} - {22.5}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {577.6}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{577.6}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {577.6}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{577.6}{35.88}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {16.1}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{577.6}{{2240.0} - {348.1} - {22.5}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{577.6}{1869.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {16.1}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 29
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {226} \quad M_{A1B1} = {37.67}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {41.33} \quad n_{A1B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {236} \quad M_{A1B2} = {39.33}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {77.33} \quad n_{A1B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {215} \quad M_{A1B3} = {35.83}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {16.83} \quad n_{A1B3} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {236} \quad M_{A2B1} = {39.33}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {29.33} \quad n_{A2B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {186} \quad M_{A2B2} = {31.0}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {64.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {201} \quad M_{A2B3} = {33.5}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {125.5} \quad n_{A2B3} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {198} \quad M_{A3B1} = {33.0}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {82.0} \quad n_{A3B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {207} \quad M_{A3B2} = {34.5}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {37.5} \quad n_{A3B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {181} \quad M_{A3B3} = {30.17}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {96.83} \quad n_{A3B3} = {6}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {54} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {53}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {54} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {45}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {66988} - \frac{1886^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {66988} - \frac{3556996}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {66988} - {65870.3}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1117.7}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {41.33 + 77.33 + 16.83 + 29.33 + 64.0 + 125.5 + 82.0 + 37.5 + 96.83}\)  \(SS_{within} = {570.65}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1117.7} - {570.65}\)  \(SS_{between} = {547.05}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {677}  \quad n_{A_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {623}  \quad n_{A_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {586}  \quad n_{A_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{677}^2}{18} + \frac{{623}^2}{18} + \frac{{586}^2}{18}} - \frac{{1886}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{458329}}{18} + \frac{{388129}}{18} + \frac{{343396}}{18}} - \frac{{3556996}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{25462.72} + {21562.72} + {19077.56}} - {65870.3}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {66103.0} - {65870.3}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {232.7}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{232.7}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {116.35}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{116.35}{12.68}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {9.18}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{232.7}{{1117.7} - {110.26} - {204.09}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{232.7}{803.35}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {45}) = {9.18}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {660}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {629}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {597}  \quad n_{B_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{660}^2}{18} + \frac{{629}^2}{18} + \frac{{597}^2}{18}} - \frac{{1886}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{435600}}{18} + \frac{{395641}}{18} + \frac{{356409}}{18}} - \frac{{3556996}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{24200.0} + {21980.06} + {19800.5}} - {65870.3}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {65980.56} - {65870.3}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {110.26}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{110.26}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {55.13}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{55.13}{12.68}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {4.35}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{110.26}{{1117.7} - {232.7} - {204.09}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{110.26}{680.91}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {45}) = {4.35}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.16}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.58}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {547.05} - {232.7} - {110.26}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {204.09}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{204.09}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {51.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{51.02}{12.68}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.02}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{204.09}{{1117.7} - {232.7} - {110.26}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{204.09}{774.74}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.26}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {45}) = {4.02}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.26}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 30
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {545} \quad M_{A1B1} = {36.33}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {791.33} \quad n_{A1B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {584} \quad M_{A1B2} = {38.93}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {394.93} \quad n_{A1B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {615} \quad M_{A2B1} = {41.0}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {224.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {670} \quad M_{A2B2} = {44.67}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {195.33} \quad n_{A2B2} = {15}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {60} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {59}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {60} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {56}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {99286} - \frac{2414^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{total} = {99286} - \frac{5827396}{60}\)  \(SS_{total} = {99286} - {97123.27}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2162.73}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {791.33 + 394.93 + 224.0 + 195.33}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1605.59}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2162.73} - {1605.59}\)  \(SS_{between} = {557.14}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1129}  \quad n_{A_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1285}  \quad n_{A_2} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1129}^2}{30} + \frac{{1285}^2}{30}} - \frac{{2414}^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1274641}}{30} + \frac{{1651225}}{30}} - \frac{{5827396}}{60}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{42488.03} + {55040.83}} - {97123.27}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {97528.86} - {97123.27}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {405.59}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{405.59}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {405.59}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{405.59}{28.67}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {14.15}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{405.59}{{2162.73} - {147.26} - {4.29}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{405.59}{2011.18}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.2}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {14.15}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.2}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1160}  \quad n_{B_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1254}  \quad n_{B_2} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1160}^2}{30} + \frac{{1254}^2}{30}} - \frac{{2414}^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1345600}}{30} + \frac{{1572516}}{30}} - \frac{{5827396}}{60}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{44853.33} + {52417.2}} - {97123.27}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {97270.53} - {97123.27}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {147.26}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{147.26}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {147.26}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{147.26}{28.67}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {5.14}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{147.26}{{2162.73} - {405.59} - {4.29}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{147.26}{1752.85}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {5.14}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.08}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.11}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {557.14} - {405.59} - {147.26}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {4.29}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{4.29}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {4.29}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{4.29}{28.67}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.15}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{4.29}{{2162.73} - {405.59} - {147.26}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{4.29}{1609.88}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {0.15}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)

\(T_{A} = {492} \quad SS_{A} = {140.18}\)  \(T_{B} = {475} \quad SS_{B} = {189.64}\)  \(T_{C} = {459} \quad SS_{C} = {304.18}\)  \(T_{D} = {395} \quad SS_{D} = {82.91}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.31}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {44} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {43}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {44} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {40}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {76571} - \frac{1821^2}{44}\]  \[SS_{total} = {76571} - \frac{3316041}{44}\]  \[SS_{total} = {76571} - {75364.57}\]  \[SS_{total} = {1206.43}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {140.18 + 189.64 + 304.18 + 82.91}\]  \[SS_{within} = {716.91}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1206.43} - {716.91}\]  \[SS_{between} = {489.52}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{489.52}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {163.17}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{716.91}{40}\]  \[MS_{within} = {17.92}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{163.17}{17.92}\]  \[F_{obt} = {9.11}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{489.52}{1206.43}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.41}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {40}) = {9.11}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.41}\)

\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{2957.78}{32}\]  \[MS_{within} = {92.43}\]

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {50} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {49}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {50} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {45}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {9874} - \frac{698^2}{50}\]  \[SS_{total} = {9874} - \frac{487204}{50}\]  \[SS_{total} = {9874} - {9744.08}\]  \[SS_{total} = {129.92}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {10.5 + 12.0 + 10.4 + 10.0 + 6.1}\]  \[SS_{within} = {49.0}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {129.92} - {49.0}\]  \[SS_{between} = {80.92}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{80.92}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {20.23}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{49.0}{45}\]  \[MS_{within} = {1.09}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{20.23}{1.09}\]  \[F_{obt} = {18.56}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{80.92}{129.92}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.62}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {45}) = {18.56}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.62}\)

title: Hypothesis Testing with Independent-Samples t-tests

Problem 1
Given the following between-subjects data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the mean of \(Group_B\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.05}\)

 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {60.45}, M_B = {44.55}\] \[SS_A = {124.73}, SS_B = {328.73}\] \[n_A = {11}, n_B = {11}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.09}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.05}, df = {20}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{124.73 + 328.73}{10 + 10}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{453.46}{20}\]  \[s_p^2 = {22.67}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{22.67}{11} + \frac{22.67}{11}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{2.06 + 2.06}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{4.12}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {2.03}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(60.45 - 44.55) - {0}}{2.03}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{15.9}{2.03}\]  \[t_{obt} = {7.83}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(60.45 - 44.55)}{{{\sqrt{22.67}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(15.9)}{4.76}\]  \[d = {3.34}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(20) = 7.83, p < 0.05, d = 3.34

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(12.29 - 14.14) - {0}}{1.33}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-1.85}{1.33}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-1.39}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(12.29 - 14.14)}{{{\sqrt{6.19}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-1.85)}{2.49}\]  \[d = {-0.74}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(12) = -1.39, p > 0.01, d = -0.74

Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{53.06}{13} + \frac{53.06}{13}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{4.08 + 4.08}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{8.16}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {2.86}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(49.31 - 44.69) - {0}}{2.86}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{4.62}{2.86}\]  \[t_{obt} = {1.62}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(49.31 - 44.69)}{{{\sqrt{53.06}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(4.62)}{7.28}\]  \[d = {0.63}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(24) = 1.62, p > 0.01, d = 0.63

State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.18}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.05}, df = {12}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{226.0 + 301.43}{6 + 6}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{527.43}{12}\]  \[s_p^2 = {43.95}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{43.95}{7} + \frac{43.95}{7}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{6.28 + 6.28}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{12.56}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {3.54}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(71.0 - 48.29) - {0}}{3.54}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{22.71}{3.54}\]  \[t_{obt} = {6.42}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(71.0 - 48.29)}{{{\sqrt{43.95}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(22.71)}{6.63}\]  \[d = {3.43}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(12) = 6.42, p < 0.05, d = 3.43

\(T_{A} = {125} \quad SS_{A} = {54.89}\)  \(T_{B} = {94} \quad SS_{B} = {60.22}\)  \(T_{C} = {129} \quad SS_{C} = {106.0}\)  \(T_{D} = {169} \quad SS_{D} = {45.56}\)  \(T_{E} = {105} \quad SS_{E} = {56.0}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.61}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {45} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {44}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {45} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {40}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {9288} - \frac{622^2}{45}\]  \[SS_{total} = {9288} - \frac{386884}{45}\]  \[SS_{total} = {9288} - {8597.42}\]  \[SS_{total} = {690.58}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {54.89 + 60.22 + 106.0 + 45.56 + 56.0}\]  \[SS_{within} = {322.67}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {690.58} - {322.67}\]  \[SS_{between} = {367.91}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{367.91}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {91.98}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{322.67}{40}\]  \[MS_{within} = {8.07}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{91.98}{8.07}\]  \[F_{obt} = {11.4}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{367.91}{690.58}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.53}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {40}) = {11.4}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.53}\)

Two-Factor ANOVA
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {442} \quad M_{A1B1} = {49.11}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {316.89} \quad n_{A1B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {450} \quad M_{A1B2} = {50.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {594.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {339} \quad M_{A2B1} = {37.67}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {320.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {327} \quad M_{A2B2} = {36.33}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {328.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {458} \quad M_{A3B1} = {50.89}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {108.89} \quad n_{A3B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {372} \quad M_{A3B2} = {41.33}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {530.0} \quad n_{A3B2} = {9}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {54} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {53}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {54} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {48}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {109738} - \frac{2388^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {109738} - \frac{5702544}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {109738} - {105602.67}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4135.33}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {316.89 + 594.0 + 320.0 + 328.0 + 108.89 + 530.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2197.78}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {4135.33} - {2197.78}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1937.55}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {892}  \quad n_{A_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {666}  \quad n_{A_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {830}  \quad n_{A_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.08}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{892}^2}{18} + \frac{{666}^2}{18} + \frac{{830}^2}{18}} - \frac{{2388}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{795664}}{18} + \frac{{443556}}{18} + \frac{{688900}}{18}} - \frac{{5702544}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{44203.56} + {24642.0} + {38272.22}} - {105602.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {107117.78} - {105602.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1515.11}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1515.11}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {757.56}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{757.56}{45.79}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {16.54}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1515.11}{{4135.33} - {149.99} - {272.45}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1515.11}{3712.89}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.41}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {16.54}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.41}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1239}  \quad n_{B_1} = {27}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1149}  \quad n_{B_2} = {27}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1239}^2}{27} + \frac{{1149}^2}{27}} - \frac{{2388}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1535121}}{27} + \frac{{1320201}}{27}} - \frac{{5702544}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{56856.33} + {48896.33}} - {105602.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {105752.66} - {105602.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {149.99}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{149.99}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {149.99}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{149.99}{45.79}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {3.28}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{149.99}{{4135.33} - {1515.11} - {272.45}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{149.99}{2347.77}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {3.28}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.08}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1937.55} - {1515.11} - {149.99}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {272.45}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{272.45}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {136.22}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{136.22}{45.79}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.97}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{272.45}{{4135.33} - {1515.11} - {149.99}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{272.45}{2470.23}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.11}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {2.97}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.11}\)

title: Hypothesis Testing with z-Scores
text:
Problem 1
Given the following data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the population mean: \(\mu = {39}\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\)





A

The necessary summary statistics for these data \[M_A = {53.6}\] \[{\sigma} = {6}\] \[n = {5}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 39\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 39\] 
The decision criteria:  \(z_{crit} = \pm{2.58}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}\) Calculate the standard error  \[\sigma_M = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{N}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{6}{\sqrt{5}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{6}{2.24}\]  \[\sigma_M = {2.68}\]  Calculate \(z_{obt}\)  \[z_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{\sigma_M}}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{53.6 - 39}{2.68}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{14.6}{2.68}\]  \[z_{obt} = {5.45}\]  Calculate Cohen’s d for effect size  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{\sigma}\]  \[d = \frac{53.6 - 39}{6}\]  \[d = \frac{14.6}{6}\]  \[d = {2.43}\]  The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  z = 5.45, p < 0.01, d = 2.43
